name: Papa Legba
shortDescription: Papa Legba is a central figure in Vodou traditions, serving as the gatekeeper between the spirit world and the human realm. He is invoked at the beginning of ceremonies to open the way for other spirits to communicate.
description: Papa Legba is one of the most important and well-known lwa (spirits) in Vodou traditions, particularly in Haitian Vodou. He serves as the intermediary between the lwa and humanity. He stands at a spiritual crossroads and grants or denies access to communication with other spirits. He is typically the first lwa invoked in any Vodou ceremony, as his presence is necessary to open the gates for the other spirits to participate. Papa Legba is often depicted as an old man, sometimes limping or using a crutch, wearing a wide-brimmed straw hat and accompanied by a dog. These depictions emphasize his age, wisdom, and connection to the earth. He is associated with crossroads, gateways, and thresholds, both literal and metaphorical. Papa Legba's origins can be traced back to West African deities, particularly those associated with pathways and transitions. His syncretism with Saint Peter, the keeper of the keys to heaven, in some forms of Vodou further solidifies his role as a gatekeeper. He is revered for his ability to remove obstacles and provide opportunities, making him a powerful figure in matters of healing, protection, and divination. Offerings to Papa Legba often include coffee, rum, tobacco, and coins.

sources.title: Divine Horsemen: The Living Gods of Haiti
sources.author: Maya Deren
